page.primary_keyword: Sala by the Sea Marbella yacht
page.title: Sala by the Sea Marbella by Yacht: How to Combine Beach Club + Sunset Cruise

data.meta_description: Sala by the Sea Marbella yacht: combine beach club luxury with a sunset cruise from €749. Dock at Puerto Banús, sip cava, and watch the sun dip over La Concha.
data.summary: Learn how to pair a Sala by the Sea Marbella yacht charter with a sunset cruise from Puerto Banús. Pricing starts at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, and snacks.
key_facts: Sala by the Sea is a beach club on the Golden Mile, accessible by yacht from Puerto Banús.
Combine a 2-hour sunset cruise with beach club entry from €749 for up to 11 guests.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (cava, wine, beer, soft drinks), and snacks.
Our Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal for this combo.
Sunset cruises typically depart Puerto Banús at 18:00 and return by 20:00 in summer.
Beach club dining and loungers are separate costs; your yacht crew can arrange a table.
No hidden fees: Spanish IVA (21%) and insurance are included in the charter price.
Book via WhatsApp for availability; pricing for larger yachts (e.g., Mangusta 80) is on request.

faq.q: What is Sala by the Sea Marbella?
faq.a: Sala by the Sea is a chic beach club and restaurant on Marbella's Golden Mile, just east of Puerto Banús. It offers Balinese-style loungers, a pool, and Mediterranean dining with direct sea views. Arriving by yacht adds a touch of glamour, and you can dock at Puerto Banús, then take a short taxi or walk to the club.

faq.q: Can I arrive at Sala by the Sea by yacht?
faq.a: Sala by the Sea does not have its own marina berths. The closest docking is at Puerto Banús, about 5 minutes by taxi or 15 minutes walk along the promenade. Your skipper will moor at Puerto Banús, and you can easily reach the beach club from there. Some guests prefer to anchor offshore and take a tender, but Puerto Banús is more convenient.

faq.q: How do I combine a sunset cruise with Sala by the Sea?
faq.a: A popular itinerary: charter a yacht from Puerto Banús at 16:00, cruise along the coast to Cabopino or Estepona, enjoy drinks and snacks, then return for sunset around 19:00–20:00. After docking, head to Sala by the Sea for dinner or cocktails. Our 2-hour sunset charter (€749) fits perfectly with a late beach club reservation.

faq.q: Which yachts are best for a beach club sunset combo?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 (9 pax, €749/2h) and Azimut 39 (11 pax, €749/2h) are ideal for small groups. For larger parties, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) or Mangusta 80 (12 pax, from €4,719/4h) offer more space. All depart from Puerto Banús and include a skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden costs. Gratuities and beach club expenses (food, drinks, loungers) are separate.

faq.q: What time does a sunset cruise depart?
faq.a: Sunset cruises in Marbella typically depart between 17:00 and 18:00, depending on the season. In summer, sunset is around 21:30, so a 2-hour cruise from 18:00 to 20:00 gives you golden-hour views. Your skipper will adjust the timing to catch the best light over La Concha mountain.

faq.q: Can I book for a special event like a birthday or proposal?
faq.a: Absolutely. Many guests use our yachts for proposals, birthdays, or anniversaries. The crew can help with decorations or a custom itinerary. Just mention your plans when booking via WhatsApp. Sala by the Sea also offers private dining options for celebrations after your cruise.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for Sala by the Sea Marbella?
faq.a: Booking is easy: send a WhatsApp message to our team with your preferred date, group size, and yacht type (e.g., Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39). We'll confirm availability and send a payment link. For Tier B yachts like the Mangusta 80, pricing is on request. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance.

data.body_html:
Imagine stepping off a luxury yacht at Puerto Banús, the sun dipping behind La Concha, and walking straight into Sala by the Sea Marbella for cocktails and dinner. This is the ultimate Marbella evening: a private sunset cruise followed by a beach club experience on the Golden Mile. Whether you're celebrating a special occasion or just want to impress guests, combining a Sala by the Sea Marbella yacht charter with a sunset cruise is effortless—and surprisingly affordable.

Why Combine a Yacht Charter with Sala by the Sea?

Sala by the Sea is one of Marbella's most stylish beach clubs, offering Balinese loungers, a heated pool, and Mediterranean cuisine. It sits right on the Golden Mile, less than 2 km from Puerto Banús. Arriving by yacht adds a sense of occasion: you cruise the Costa del Sol, anchor offshore for a dip (weather permitting), then dock at Puerto Banús and head to the club. The contrast of open sea and beach-club luxury is unforgettable.

Best Yachts for a Sunset Cruise + Beach Club Combo

Our fleet has several options perfect for this itinerary. For small groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal—both start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. For larger parties, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers more space and a flybridge for sunset views. If you want to make a real statement, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is available from €4,719 for 4 hours, with a jet ski included free. All depart from Puerto Banús.

Suggested Itinerary: Sunset Cruise + Sala by the Sea

  • 16:00 – Board your yacht at Puerto Banús. Meet your skipper and enjoy welcome drinks.
  • 16:30–18:30 – Cruise east towards Cabopino or west towards Estepona. Stop for a swim at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés.
  • 18:30–20:00 – Head back towards Puerto Banús as the sun sets. Sip cava and watch the colours change over La Concha.
  • 20:00 – Dock at Puerto Banús. Walk or taxi (5 min) to Sala by the Sea for dinner or cocktails.

This itinerary works perfectly with a 2-hour charter. If you want more time on the water, book a 4-hour charter and add a stop at a beach club for lunch.

Pricing and Inclusions

Our Tier A yachts (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) are €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B (Mangusta 80 and variants) starts at €4,719 for 4 hours. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Beach club expenses—loungers, food, drinks—are separate. For other yachts, pricing is on request via WhatsApp.

What to Expect at Sala by the Sea

Sala by the Sea offers a relaxed but upscale vibe. You can book a sun lounger (around €50–€100 per day, depending on season) or reserve a table for dinner. The menu features fresh seafood, sushi, and grilled meats, with cocktails like their signature Sala Spritz. The dress code is smart-casual; no beachwear inside the restaurant. Your yacht crew can help arrange a reservation—just ask when booking.

Tips for a Seamless Experience

  • Book in advance – Yachts and beach club tables fill up quickly, especially in July and August. Contact us via WhatsApp at least 48 hours ahead.
  • Check the weather – Summer evenings are usually calm, but afternoon winds can pick up. Your skipper will advise on the best route.
  • Pack light – Bring a light jacket for the evening breeze, sunscreen, and a swimsuit if you plan to swim.
  • Timing – Sunset in Marbella varies from 18:30 (winter) to 21:30 (summer). Confirm your charter time with us to catch the golden hour.

For more ideas on Marbella yacht charters, explore our full fleet and itineraries.

Frequently asked questions

What is Sala by the Sea Marbella?

Sala by the Sea is a chic beach club and restaurant on Marbella's Golden Mile, just east of Puerto Banús. It offers Balinese-style loungers, a pool, and Mediterranean dining with direct sea views. Arriving by yacht adds a touch of glamour, and you can dock at Puerto Banús, then take a short taxi or walk to the club.

Can I arrive at Sala by the Sea by yacht?

Sala by the Sea does not have its own marina berths. The closest docking is at Puerto Banús, about 5 minutes by taxi or 15 minutes walk along the promenade. Your skipper will moor at Puerto Banús, and you can easily reach the beach club from there. Some guests prefer to anchor offshore and take a tender, but Puerto Banús is more convenient.

A popular itinerary: charter a yacht from Puerto Banús at 16:00, cruise along the coast to Cabopino or Estepona, enjoy drinks and snacks, then return for sunset around 19:00–20:00. After docking, head to Sala by the Sea for dinner or cocktails. Our 2-hour sunset charter (€749) fits perfectly with a late beach club reservation.

The Astondoa 40 (9 pax, €749/2h) and Azimut 39 (11 pax, €749/2h) are ideal for small groups. For larger parties, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) or Mangusta 80 (12 pax, from €4,719/4h) offer more space. All depart from Puerto Banús and include a skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden costs. Gratuities and beach club expenses (food, drinks, loungers) are separate.

What time does a sunset cruise depart?

Sunset cruises in Marbella typically depart between 17:00 and 18:00, depending on the season. In summer, sunset is around 21:30, so a 2-hour cruise from 18:00 to 20:00 gives you golden-hour views. Your skipper will adjust the timing to catch the best light over La Concha mountain.

Can I book for a special event like a birthday or proposal?

Absolutely. Many guests use our yachts for proposals, birthdays, or anniversaries. The crew can help with decorations or a custom itinerary. Just mention your plans when booking via WhatsApp. Sala by the Sea also offers private dining options for celebrations after your cruise.

How do I book a yacht for Sala by the Sea Marbella?

Booking is easy: send a WhatsApp message to our team with your preferred date, group size, and yacht type (e.g., Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39). We'll confirm availability and send a payment link. For Tier B yachts like the Mangusta 80, pricing is on request. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance.
